findings.description: Pre-LLM zh-TW dominance check writes shops.processing_status='failed' + rejection_reason BEFORE the check_job_still_claimed guard at line 100. If admin force-fails the job mid-LLM-call and the LLM returns non-zh output, the handler silently overwrites the operator's cancel_reason. Move guard before the non-zh branch or re-check guard inside it.
findings.resolution: Moved check_job_still_claimed guard before the zh-TW branch. Commit 2fd907e.

findings.description: Exception handler writes shops.processing_status='failed' + rejection_reason=f'Enrichment error: {exc}' unconditionally, without consulting the cancel guard. Any exception after an admin cancel (network error, LLM 500) will silently overwrite the operator's cancel state — exactly the regression DEV-311 intends to prevent. Run check_job_still_claimed in the except path before writing.
findings.resolution: Added guard check in except block: `if not _failure_recorded and (job_id is None or check_job_still_claimed(db, job_id))`. Commit 2fd907e.

findings.description: Same DEV-311 regression as enrich_shop: the except block unconditionally writes shops.processing_status='failed' + rejection_reason when should_advance is true, bypassing the cancel guard. Any mid-flight exception after admin cancel overwrites the operator reason.
findings.resolution: Added guard check in except block: `if should_advance and (job_id is None or check_job_still_claimed(db, job_id))`. Commit 6371646.

findings.description: STATUS_OPTIONS omits the new 'cancelled' status. Plan decision #2 makes 'cancelled' a first-class distinct status for operator force-fails — but operators cannot filter by it in the UI. Add 'cancelled' to STATUS_OPTIONS.

Migration adds failed_at TIMESTAMPTZ but no backend code ever writes to it (verified: grep failed_at in backend/ only finds the migration + an unrelated 'failed_at_stage' field). Either drop the column or populate it in queue.fail() when transitioning to failed/dead_letter. Current state = dead column.
findings.resolution: Added failed_at write in JobQueue.fail() when status transitions to FAILED. Commit c4087f6.

findings.description: ConfirmDialog copy: 'This cannot be undone. The job will be moved to dead letter.' Backend now transitions to 'cancelled' (per plan decision #2), not dead_letter. Update copy to match actual behavior.
findings.resolution: Updated copy to 'This cannot be undone. The job will be cancelled.' Commit 5f2f872.

findings.description: job_logs.job_id UUID NOT NULL has no foreign key reference to job_queue(id). Deleting a job leaves orphan log rows with no integrity guarantee. Add REFERENCES job_queue(id) ON DELETE CASCADE.
findings.resolution: Added REFERENCES job_queue(id) ON DELETE CASCADE to job_logs.job_id. Commit 343cfd6.

findings.description: get_job_logs accepts after_ts: str | None and passes it directly to Supabase .gt('created_at', after_ts) without validation. A malformed value surfaces as an opaque Postgres error. Validate as ISO-8601 datetime via Pydantic Query or datetime.fromisoformat before use.
findings.resolution: Added datetime.fromisoformat() validation with HTTP 422 response on failure. Commit c4c6d10.

findings.description: Guard placement consistency: ValueError at line 86 is raised before the guard check at line 88, meaning a mid-flight cancel during a non-zh response still propagates to the except block at line 121 which logs job.error. Lower impact than enrich_shop (no shops write) but the overall pattern — guard must wrap all write/error paths — needs a consistent refactor across all three handlers.
findings.resolution: Moved check_job_still_claimed guard before the zh-TW ValueError raise for consistent pattern across all handlers. Commit 213b452.

findings.description: check_job_still_claimed is a non-atomic TOCTOU check: read status, then unconditional UPDATE in the handler. Race window is small but real. Consider a conditional UPDATE on shops that only succeeds when job_queue.status='claimed' (via CTE or a single RPC), matching the pattern already used in cancel_job. Plan decision #4 explicitly accepts this tradeoff, so classified debatable.
findings.resolution: Accepted per plan decision #4 — TOCTOU tradeoff explicitly accepted. No code change needed. The plan explicitly documents this as an accepted design tradeoff.

findings.description: Plan spec specifies `async def log_job_event(...)`; implementation is sync `def`. Callers work because no `await` is used, but inconsistency with plan + other worker helpers is surprising. Either update plan doc or make it async.
findings.resolution: Made log_job_event async and updated all callers (handlers + admin.py) to await it. Updated test_job_log.py to use async test functions. Commit ae6efc2.

findings.description: Tests patch workers.job_guard.get_status — an internal function of the module under test — instead of mocking at the DB boundary. Violates CLAUDE.md 'mock at boundaries only' testing principle. Should inject a mocked DB client and let check_job_still_claimed call real get_status.
findings.resolution: Refactored tests to pass a mocked DB client and removed internal get_status patches. check_job_still_claimed now exercises real get_status via DB mock. Commit a5c4f35.

findings.description: Test descriptions use function-name framing ('check_job_still_claimed returns True when claimed') instead of user-action/outcome framing required by CLAUDE.md. Rewrite as e.g. 'Given a worker whose job was cancelled mid-flight, when the guard runs, it reports the job is no longer claimed.' Applies to test_job_log.py line 6, 25 as well.
findings.resolution: Rewrote all test descriptions in test_job_guard.py and test_job_log.py to describe worker/operator outcomes. Commits a5c4f35, ae6efc2.

findings.description: Fetch inside useEffect has no AbortController. If component unmounts mid-fetch, the response is discarded but may trigger a setState-after-unmount warning in strict mode. Low impact since polling is short-lived. Add AbortController for hygiene.
findings.resolution: Added AbortController, pass signal to fetch, and call controller.abort() in cleanup. Commit 469773b.

findings.description: New get_status helper is a module-level function while other queue operations are methods on JobQueue. Inconsistent API shape — prefer a method on JobQueue or document why the helper sits outside.
findings.resolution: Added get_status as a method on JobQueue. Kept module-level function as a deprecated shim for existing callers (job_guard.py uses it directly). Commit c4087f6.
